assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items discuss U.S.-Iran conflict developments (including effects in UAE/Dubai), but there is no AWS public post-incident report attributing a Middle East region/AZ disruption to conflict-related physical attack or strikes.

No regulatory filing (8-K/6-K/equivalent) is cited in today’s news stating that Gulf/Middle East cloud disruption materially disrupted a public company’s operations or revenue recognition for at least one day.

Today’s reporting references military events (e.g., downed jet, threats), but there is no DoD/CENTCOM announcement of newly arrived or repositioned named air-and-missile defense assets to the Gulf explicitly citing Iranian missile/drone threats.
No announcement from Amazon/Microsoft/Google is included today describing a Middle East cloud resilience change explicitly referencing conflict risk and naming a Gulf/Middle East region.

There is no evidence in today’s news of a BIS Final Rule or updated export-control guidance further restricting AI accelerator/server exports beyond the 2026-03-31 baseline, nor an explicit China-targeting reference.
No Supreme Court certiorari grant is reported today in a Section 230-related case focused on algorithmic recommendations or platform design choices.

Today’s items do not mention any U.S.-based data-center/AI infrastructure developer reaching FID or signing a power-supply agreement for ≥500 MW of dedicated on-site natural-gas generation for AI/data-center loads.
No FDA action is reported today granting a Breakthrough Therapy Designation (BTD) or RMAT designation to a gene therapy intended to restore/improve hearing in sensorineural hearing loss.

Today’s news bullets do not include a G7 national security/defense strategy update that names quantum computing as a near-term cryptographic threat and commits to a post-quantum migration deadline by 2030 or earlier.
